title: [LLHD] Empty llhd.proc assertion failure
body:
Running `circt-opt --convert-llhd-to-llvm` on an emtpy `llhd.proc` produces: `Assertion !NodePtr->isKnownSentinel() failed.`

This input isn't valid according to the docs ([llhd.proc](https://circt.llvm.org/docs/Dialects/LLHD/#llhdproc-circtllhdprocop)) because `llhd.proc` must be terminated with either `llhd.wait` or a `llhd.halt`, but I was wondering shouldn't this be caught by the MLIR [Verifier.cpp](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/167b90d0401d0fe488195c7e3d6fc1edc8fc5d94/mlir/lib/IR/Verifier.cpp#L155) and emit a `block with no terminator` error?

Commit: 9a78825b45566cf0abbdb6650ccd3954b2545927
Command: `circt-opt --convert-llhd-to-llvm`
Input:
```mlir
module {
  llhd.proc @empty() -> () {
  }
}
```
Trace:
```
circt-opt: /workdir/circt/llvm/llvm/include/llvm/ADT/ilist_iterator.h:138: llvm::ilist_iterator::reference llvm::ilist_iterator<llvm::ilist_detail::node_options<mlir::Block, true, false, void, false>, false, false>::operator*() const [OptionsT = llvm::ilist_detail::node_options<mlir::Block, true, false, void, false>, IsReverse = false, IsConst = false]: Assertion `!NodePtr->isKnownSentinel()' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/circt and include the crash backtrace.
Stack dump:
0.	Program arguments: /workdir/circt/build/bin/circt-opt --convert-llhd-to-llvm llhd_proc_empty.mlir

 #5 0x00007f60eaf3f476 gsignal (/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x42476)
 #6 0x00007f60eaf257f3 abort (/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x287f3)
```
